docs.title: captchakit
docs.text: Async-first, fully type-hinted, minimal captcha library for Python 3.10+. Zero runtime dependencies beyond Pillow. Drop-in adapters for FastAPI, aiogram and discord.py.

docs.title: Why
docs.text: Most Python captcha libraries stop at "render a PNG" — no TTL, no attempt tracking, no async, partial type hints, and no framework glue. captchakit gives you a small modern SDK that handles the whole issue → verify → expire flow, and ships adapters so you're wired into your framework in a single line.

docs.title: At a glance
docs.text: lepture/captcha claptcha multicolorcaptcha captchakit Async API ❌ ❌ ❌ ✅ py.typed + mypy strict ⚠️ ❌ ❌ ✅ TTL & attempt tracking ❌ ❌ ❌ ✅ built-in Pluggable storage ❌ ❌ ❌ ✅ Protocol Framework adapters ❌ ❌ ❌ ✅ FastAPI · aiogram · discord.py

docs.title: 30-second example
docs.text:
import asyncio
from captchakit import (
    CaptchaManager, ImageRenderer, MemoryStorage, TextChallengeFactory,
)

async def main() -> None:
    manager = CaptchaManager(
        factory=TextChallengeFactory(length=5),
        renderer=ImageRenderer(),
        storage=MemoryStorage(),
        ttl=120.0,
        max_attempts=3,
    )
    challenge_id, png_bytes = await manager.issue()
    # ... show png_bytes to the user, receive their answer ...
    ok = await manager.verify(challenge_id, user_input="ABCDE")
    print("verified" if ok else "wrong answer, more attempts remain")

asyncio.run(main())

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
pip install captchakit                 # core
pip install "captchakit[fastapi]"      # + FastAPI adapter
pip install "captchakit[aiogram]"      # + aiogram adapter
pip install "captchakit[discord]"      # + discord.py adapter
pip install "captchakit[redis]"        # + Redis storage backend

docs.title: Security scope
docs.text: captchakit is a lightweight human-check, not a bot-farm-grade security layer. For high-value surfaces (login, payment, password reset) use hCaptcha / Cloudflare Turnstile / reCAPTCHA Enterprise in addition. See SECURITY.md for the full threat model.

docs.title: captchakit.manager.CaptchaManager dataclass
docs.text:
Coordinates a :class:ChallengeFactory, :class:Renderer and :class:Storage.

Typical usage::

manager = CaptchaManager(
    factory=TextChallengeFactory(),
    renderer=ImageRenderer(),
    storage=MemoryStorage(),
    ttl=120.0,
)
challenge_id, png = await manager.issue()
...
ok = await manager.verify(challenge_id, user_input)

docs.title: discard async
docs.text:
discard(challenge_id: str) -> None

Remove a challenge without checking it (e.g. on user cancel).

docs.title: issue async
docs.text:
issue() -> tuple[str, bytes]

Create and persist a fresh challenge, return (id, image_bytes).

docs.title: verify async
docs.text:
verify(challenge_id: str, user_input: str) -> bool

Check a user's answer.

Returns:

Type Description bool

True if the answer matches (the challenge is then deleted),

bool

False if the answer is wrong but the user still has attempts left.

Raises:

Type Description ChallengeNotFound

No such challenge (already consumed, never issued, or evicted by TTL).

ChallengeExpired

The challenge passed its expiration time.

TooManyAttempts

This was the final attempt and it was wrong.

docs.title: captchakit.challenges.base.Challenge dataclass
docs.text: A complete captcha challenge stored by the manager.

docs.title: check
docs.text:
check(user_input: str) -> bool

Constant-time comparison against the expected solution.

docs.title: captchakit.challenges.base.ChallengeSpec dataclass
docs.text:
A challenge's raw content before an id/TTL is attached.

Produced by a :class:ChallengeFactory; the :class:~captchakit.CaptchaManager wraps it into a :class:Challenge with identifier and timestamps.

docs.title: captchakit.challenges.base.ChallengeFactory
docs.text:
Bases: Protocol

Produces fresh :class:ChallengeSpec instances on demand.

docs.title: captchakit.challenges.text.TextChallengeFactory dataclass
docs.text:
Produces random alphanumeric strings.

The default charset omits visually ambiguous characters (I, O, 0, 1) so a user isn't penalised for font choice.

docs.title: captchakit.challenges.math.MathChallengeFactory dataclass
docs.text:
Produces easy arithmetic puzzles such as "7 + 3 = ?".

Results of subtractions are always non-negative: operands are swapped when needed so the user never has to deal with negative numbers.

docs.title: captchakit.challenges.grid.EmojiGridChallengeFactory dataclass
docs.text:
Produces a single-pick emoji-grid challenge.

One target emoji is placed in a randomly chosen cell; the remaining cells are filled with distractors drawn from emoji_pool. The user's expected answer is the 1-indexed cell number as a string (e.g. "3").

docs.title: captchakit.challenges.word.WordChallengeFactory dataclass
docs.text:
Produces a single random word from a wordlist.

The default list avoids visually / phonetically ambiguous words and ships ~50 common English nouns. Pass your own words tuple to localise or narrow the pool.

docs.title: captchakit.renderers.base.Renderer
docs.text:
Bases: Protocol

Turns a :class:Challenge into bytes (typically an image).

docs.title: captchakit.renderers.image.ImageRenderer dataclass
docs.text:
Renders a challenge's prompt into a PNG.

The renderer is intentionally simple: it draws the prompt glyph-by-glyph with small random rotations / offsets and a few decorative lines. It is not meant to defeat OCR — it's a light human-check layer (see SECURITY notes in the README).

docs.title: captchakit.storage.base.Storage
docs.text:
Bases: Protocol

Persists challenges while they are live.

Implementations MUST be safe under concurrent async access from a single event loop. Multi-process safety is the backend's own responsibility (e.g. Redis is multi-process-safe; in-memory is not).

docs.title: captchakit.storage.memory.MemoryStorage dataclass
docs.text:
Dict-based storage with a single :class:asyncio.Lock.

Suitable for single-process deployments and tests. For multi-process setups (e.g. gunicorn with multiple workers) use a shared backend such as :class:~captchakit.storage.redis.RedisStorage (optional extra).

docs.title: cleanup_expired async
docs.text:
cleanup_expired() -> int

Remove every challenge whose expires_at is in the past.

Returns the number of entries evicted. Safe to call from a background task; intended to be scheduled periodically by the host application.

docs.title: captchakit.storage.redis.RedisStorage dataclass
docs.text:
Stores challenges in Redis using native TTL.

Each challenge uses two keys:

  • <prefix>:ch:<id> — JSON-encoded challenge, expires at challenge.expires_at.
  • <prefix>:att:<id> — attempt counter, same expiry.

incr_attempts is a best-effort atomic check — it returns 0 if the challenge key was already evicted (by TTL or explicit delete) at the moment of the call.

docs.title: captchakit.errors
docs.text: Exception hierarchy for captchakit.

docs.title: CaptchaKitError
docs.text:
Bases: Exception

Base class for all captchakit exceptions.

docs.title: ChallengeError
docs.text:
Bases: CaptchaKitError

Base for errors that reference a specific challenge id.

Catch this to handle :class:ChallengeNotFound, :class:ChallengeExpired and :class:TooManyAttempts uniformly.

docs.title: ChallengeExpired
docs.text:
Bases: ChallengeError

The challenge has passed its expiration time.

docs.title: ChallengeNotFound
docs.text:
Bases: ChallengeError

The referenced challenge id does not exist in storage.

docs.title: StorageError
docs.text:
Bases: CaptchaKitError

Underlying storage backend failed.

docs.title: TooManyAttempts
docs.text:
Bases: ChallengeError

The allowed number of verification attempts has been exceeded.

docs.title: 1. Install
docs.text: pip install "captchakit[fastapi]"

docs.title: 2. Wire up a manager
docs.text:
from captchakit import (
    CaptchaManager,
    ImageRenderer,
    MathChallengeFactory,
    MemoryStorage,
)

manager = CaptchaManager(
    factory=MathChallengeFactory(),
    renderer=ImageRenderer(),
    storage=MemoryStorage(),
    ttl=120.0,         # seconds a challenge stays valid
    max_attempts=3,    # wrong answers before invalidation
)

docs.title: 3. Issue and verify
docs.text:
challenge_id, png_bytes = await manager.issue()
# ...deliver png_bytes to the user, collect their answer...
try:
    ok = await manager.verify(challenge_id, "7")
except captchakit.ChallengeExpired:
    ...  # TTL elapsed
except captchakit.TooManyAttempts:
    ...  # user burned through their guesses

verify returns True on success, False while the user still has attempts remaining. Specific failure modes raise typed exceptions.

docs.title: Choosing a challenge factory
docs.text: Factory Output Best for TextChallengeFactory random 5-char string baseline MathChallengeFactory e.g. "7 + 3 = ?" form sign-ups EmojiGridChallengeFactory grid + target emoji Telegram/Discord inline buttons WordChallengeFactory real word from wordlist accessibility-friendly

docs.title: Choosing a storage backend
docs.text:
• MemoryStorage — single-process apps, tests. Fast, zero setup.
  • RedisStorage — multi-worker deployments, horizontal scale. Native Redis TTL handles cleanup automatically. Opt-in via the [redis] extra.
from redis.asyncio import Redis
from captchakit.storage import RedisStorage

storage = RedisStorage(client=Redis.from_url("redis://localhost:6379"))

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
• Integrate with your framework: FastAPI · aiogram · Discord
  • Browse the API reference.

docs.title: Storage backends
docs.text:
All storage backends implement the Storage protocol (see API reference):

async def put(self, challenge: Challenge) -> None: ...
async def get(self, challenge_id: str) -> Challenge | None: ...
async def delete(self, challenge_id: str) -> None: ...
async def incr_attempts(self, challenge_id: str) -> int: ...

Swap in your own implementation (e.g. Postgres, Memcached, DynamoDB) by writing four async methods — no subclassing required.

docs.title: MemoryStorage
docs.text:
from captchakit import MemoryStorage

storage = MemoryStorage()
  • In-process dict + asyncio.Lock.
  • Fast, zero config.
  • Not multi-process safe — use Redis once you run more than one worker.
  • Provides an extra cleanup_expired() coroutine for periodic eviction.

docs.title: RedisStorage
docs.text:
from redis.asyncio import Redis
from captchakit.storage import RedisStorage

client = Redis.from_url("redis://localhost:6379")
storage = RedisStorage(client=client, prefix="myapp")
  • Async client (redis.asyncio.Redis).
  • Two keys per challenge under <prefix>:ch:<id> and <prefix>:att:<id>.
  • Native Redis TTL (SETEX) evicts expired challenges automatically — no background task required.
  • Multi-process and multi-host safe; use this for any deployment with more than one worker.
  • incr_attempts is best-effort atomic — if the challenge key was already evicted at the moment of the call, it returns 0.

docs.title: Opt-in
docs.text:
pip install "captchakit[redis]"

Until you install the extra, from captchakit.storage import RedisStorage defers the underlying redis import — so the absence of the package never breaks from captchakit.storage import MemoryStorage.

docs.title: aiogram adapter
docs.text:
Install: pip install "captchakit[aiogram]".

The adapter exposes a single thin helper — send_captcha — which issues a challenge, uploads the rendered PNG to Telegram via bot.send_photo, and returns the challenge id. State management (pairing the id with the user's eventual reply) belongs to your FSM.

docs.title: Helper
docs.text:
from captchakit.adapters.aiogram import send_captcha

challenge_id = await send_captcha(
    bot,
    chat_id=user_id,
    manager=manager,
    caption="Solve this to join:",   # optional
    filename="captcha.png",          # optional
)

docs.title: End-to-end: chat-join verification
docs.text:
from aiogram import F
from aiogram.fsm.context import FSMContext
from captchakit import CaptchaManager, ImageRenderer, MathChallengeFactory, MemoryStorage
from captchakit.adapters.aiogram import send_captcha

manager = CaptchaManager(
    MathChallengeFactory(), ImageRenderer(), MemoryStorage(),
)

@dp.chat_join_request()
async def on_join(req, state: FSMContext):
    cid = await send_captcha(req.bot, req.from_user.id, manager)
    await state.update_data(captcha_id=cid, join_chat_id=req.chat.id)

@dp.message(F.text)
async def on_reply(msg, state: FSMContext):
    data = await state.get_data()
    cid = data.get("captcha_id")
    if cid is None:
        return
    if await manager.verify(cid, msg.text):
        await msg.bot.approve_chat_join_request(
            data["join_chat_id"], msg.from_user.id,
        )
        await state.clear()

docs.title: Discord adapter
docs.text:
Install: pip install "captchakit[discord]".

Thin helper — send_captcha — issues a challenge, uploads the PNG as a discord.File attachment, and returns the challenge id. Coordinating that id with the user's later reply is your bot's job (a plain dict keyed by member.id works fine; use Redis once you scale to multiple processes).

docs.title: Helper
docs.text:
from captchakit.adapters.discord import send_captcha

challenge_id = await send_captcha(
    channel,               # anything Messageable: DMChannel, TextChannel, Thread
    manager,
    content="Prove you're human:",  # optional
    filename="captcha.png",         # optional
)

docs.title: End-to-end: on-join DM verification
docs.text:
import discord
from discord.ext import commands
from captchakit import CaptchaManager, ImageRenderer, MathChallengeFactory, MemoryStorage
from captchakit.adapters.discord import send_captcha

intents = discord.Intents.default()
intents.members = True
intents.message_content = True

bot = commands.Bot(command_prefix="!", intents=intents)
manager = CaptchaManager(
    MathChallengeFactory(), ImageRenderer(), MemoryStorage(),
)
pending: dict[int, str] = {}

@bot.event
async def on_member_join(member: discord.Member) -> None:
    dm = await member.create_dm()
    pending[member.id] = await send_captcha(dm, manager)

@bot.event
async def on_message(msg: discord.Message) -> None:
    cid = pending.get(msg.author.id)
    if cid is None or msg.guild is not None:
        return
    if await manager.verify(cid, msg.content):
        del pending[msg.author.id]
        await msg.channel.send("Welcome!")

docs.title: FastAPI adapter
docs.text: Install: pip install "captchakit[fastapi]".

docs.title: Router + dependency
docs.text:
from fastapi import Depends, FastAPI
from captchakit import (
    CaptchaManager, ImageRenderer, MathChallengeFactory, MemoryStorage,
)
from captchakit.adapters.fastapi import captcha_router, verify_captcha

manager = CaptchaManager(
    MathChallengeFactory(), ImageRenderer(), MemoryStorage(),
)
app = FastAPI()
app.include_router(captcha_router(manager, prefix="/captcha"))

@app.post("/register")
async def register(_: None = Depends(verify_captcha(manager))) -> dict[str, bool]:
    return {"ok": True}

docs.title: Exposed endpoints
docs.text: Method Path Body / Response POST {prefix}/new {"id": str, "image_url": str} GET {prefix}/{id}.png PNG bytes (image/png) POST {prefix}/verify form id, answer → {"ok": bool}

docs.title: verify_captcha dependency
docs.text:
Expects two form fields on the protected route:

  • captcha_id — the id returned by /new
  • captcha_answer — the user's answer

On failure, it raises a typed HTTPException:

Exception Status ChallengeNotFound 404 ChallengeExpired 410 TooManyAttempts 429 (wrong answer, still has attempts) 403

docs.title: Full demo
docs.text:
See examples/fastapi_login.py in the repo — run with:

uv run python -m uvicorn examples.fastapi_login:app --reload
